title: machaeranthera frutescens
intro: Machaeranthera frutescens, also known as White Sagebrush, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Machaeranthera frutescens include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antiviral and antibacterial properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in aromatherapy due to its pleasant, earthy scent.
